questions: You're a secure, trusting person who doesn't worry about losing what's yours. Your partner mentions that an attractive coworker has been joining them for lunch every day. How do you respond?

You're a secure, trusting person who doesn't worry about losing what's yours. A new hire at work is getting a lot of praise from your boss, who used to single you out as the star performer. How do you react?

You're a secure, trusting person who doesn't worry about losing what's yours. Your best friend starts spending a lot of time with someone new and cancels plans with you twice in a row. What do you do?
You're a secure, trusting person who doesn't worry about losing what's yours. Your partner's ex starts liking all their social media posts and leaving comments. How do you handle it?
You're a secure, trusting person who doesn't worry about losing what's yours. At a party, someone is being very flirtatious with your partner right in front of you. How do you react?
You're a secure, trusting person who doesn't worry about losing what's yours. Your child starts saying that their stepparent is 'cooler' than you. What do you do?

You're a secure, trusting person who doesn't worry about losing what's yours. A younger colleague is assigned to shadow you and your manager starts giving them some of your key responsibilities. How do you respond?
You're a secure, trusting person who doesn't worry about losing what's yours. Your partner gets a text late at night, laughs, and puts their phone face down without explaining. What do you do?
You're a secure, trusting person who doesn't worry about losing what's yours. Your closest work ally starts having private meetings with a rival team lead. How do you react?

You're a secure, trusting person who doesn't worry about losing what's yours. Your sibling announces they're moving closer to your parents, while you live far away and have always been the primary caretaker. How do you respond?
